draft.introduction: Krishna declares that he pervades the entire universe in his unmanifest form. All beings exist in him, yet he is not contained in them—the supreme paradox of divine transcendence and immanence.

translation.text: By me all this universe is pervaded in my unmanifest form. All beings exist in me, but I do not dwell in them. And yet beings do not exist in me—behold my divine mystery! My Self sustains all beings but does not dwell in them.
translation.original_text: mayā tatam idaṃ sarvaṃ jagad avyakta-mūrtinā | mat-sthāni sarva-bhūtāni na cāhaṃ teṣv avasthitaḥ

translator_notes.note: The consecutive assertions “all beings exist in me” and “beings do not exist in me” constitute a deliberate paradox. Rāmānuja resolves it by distinguishing God’s essence from God’s sustaining power; śaṅkara reads it as pointing beyond conceptual thought entirely.
